programmes.show_description: Blues. The bikers head to New Orleans, where they meet blues musician Little Freddie King and share their own take on a traditional, Southern classic - the po' boy sandwich. S1, Ep2
Rock And Roll. The duo follow in the footsteps of music legends and sample traditional Tennessean dishes at a classic diner. Next, they visit Elvis Presley's mansion in Memphis. S1, Ep3
Flamenco And Capture The Flag. Buddy flaunts his dance moves when he designs a flamenco cake. Meanwhile, Mauro and the team make a cake for a nearby summer camp, and it gets messy! S9, Ep3
Color Run And Back To School. The team creates a cake to mark an event for the Mayor of Newark providing school supplies for kids. Madeline makes a rainbow cake for a charity run! S9, Ep4
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.
Meat And Heat. On a trip to Houston, Guy stops by a barbecue joint that's cooking brisket tacos and jalapeno sausage. Plus, he tries bulgogi at a Korean spot in Tennessee. S34, Ep13
Hearty Handfuls. Guy Fieri digs into hearty handfuls of major flavour, like next-level pizza in Tennessee, tasty tacos in Alberta, and elevated empanadas in Georgia. S38, Ep12
Eating Up New Orleans. Guy Fieri is taking a tasty tour of New Orleans, Louisiana. He stops at a seafood restaurant that serves up creative dishes and tries potato salad with a twist. S29, Ep13
Soup, Sandwiches And Seafood. Guy Fieri dives into amped-up eats, from Nashville hot chicken at a coastal Georgia joint and savoury Vietnamese street food in a Calgary noodle bar. S38, Ep14
Buon Appetito. Ina creates a classic Italian Bolognese, but she updates it to be an easy weeknight dinner. Then, she makes a traditional Tuscan roasted sausage and grapes dish. S5, Ep3
Market Day. Ina is inspired by local produce and embraces the incredible ingredients that she can find on her doorstep. She makes honey vanilla pound cake and corn and Brie salad. S5, Ep4
Make It Fast. Ina shows how to make two tasty main-course ideas quickly. The perfect ideas for dinner parties; roasted salmon with green herbs and chicken with shallots. S5, Ep5
Post Cards From Paris. Ina enjoys a day of cooking her favourite French meals, including an omelette and veal chops with Roquefort butter and potato basil purée. S5, Ep6
Barefoot Contessa Burger Joint. Ina makes burgers with a twist. She gives the sides a makeover with a buttermilk ranch dressing on bibb lettuce and sweet potato chips. S5, Ep7
How The Cookie Crumbles. Ina bakes a double delicious treat - chocolate chunk blondies and cookies using one easy batter. Plus, Patti Page shows Ina how to make animal cookies. S5, Ep8
Just Candy. Ina hits the sweet spot with her favourite candy recipes, including squishy marshmallows and white chocolate bark. Then she makes a warming squash soup. S5, Ep9
Shake It Up. Ina prepares three easy canapes that are perfect for the holiday season including, salmon tartare. Also, mixologist Brian Van Flandern makes two delicious cocktails. S5, Ep10
Miguel While You Were Out. Ina cooks for her friend, Miguel Flores-Vianaa. She makes panko-crusted salmon, warm French lentils, an all-American cheeseboard and chicken noodle soup. S6, Ep1
Drill Dinner. Ina serves a delicious dinner at the fire station for hungry firefighters after a practice drill. She makes beef barley soup, sloppy Joes and pecan caramel sundaes. S6, Ep2
James arrives in the tiny village of Giverny, known for its food and art. He explores Monet's kitchen and cooks up an autumnal treat of apple doughnuts. S1, Ep17
James ventures to Paris, one of his favourite cities in the world! He cooks duck breast with tangy vegetables, a steak baguette and gin and cucumber savarin. S1, Ep18
The Bikers create their version of hearty pub classics. They give a new look to a traditional pudding and reveal the secrets behind the ultimate fishcake. S1, Ep5
The Bikers cook dishes that will bring smiles to the table. They make their epic biker burger, a show-stopping meringue, and a modern take on a classic banana split. S1, Ep6
The Bikers use top-notch ingredients to create amazing dishes. They update the classic rum baba and use a great cut of beef in a traditional Greek stifado. S1, Ep7
The Bikers get nostalgic with recipes to conjure up happy memories. They make a Lancashire hotpot like Dave's mum used to make, and a French speciality for Si. S1, Ep8
Long Island. Adam Richman is on Long Island, New York, for a massive plate of Italian food, a colossal gelato sundae and the Davey Jones' Locker Challenge. S2, Ep28
Oklahoma City. Adam Richman is in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, for a presidential T-bone steak, the Sooner State original fried onion burger and a Fried Catfish Challenge. S2, Ep29
Bari. Adam's in Bari tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including focaccia Barese and a local seafood dish called tiella Barese. S1, Ep9
Rome. Adam's in Rome tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including pasta dish cacio e pepe, and porchetta Romana: suckling pig Rome style. S1, Ep10
Flavortown Finds. Guy Fieri's getting into major flavour with bagels in Gainesville, crawfish and oysters in Baton Rouge, and pastrami and gnocchi in Naples. S41, Ep5
Sandwich Shenanigans. Guy Fieri's digging into big-time barbecue in Dallas, tries a spot-on Cubano in Gainesville, and eats a rocking po'boy in Baton Rouge. S41, Ep6
Bird, Belly And Beef. Guy Fieri seeks out flavourful meat! He checks out Mexican pork sandwiches in Memphis, Tennessee, and rich short ribs in St. Simons Island, Georgia. S38, Ep16
Meat And Heat. On a trip to Houston, Guy stops by a barbecue joint that's cooking brisket tacos and jalapeno sausage. Plus, he tries bulgogi at a Korean spot in Tennessee. S34, Ep13
Sandwich-Fest. Guy Fieri checks out how stellar sandwiches stack up with a chicken trifecta in Nebraska, dynamite dip in Alabama and bomb bao in Connecticut. S38, Ep6
Meaty To Meatless. Guy Fieri digs into dishes with and without meat, like righteous rabbit in Alabama, meatless chicken in Nebraska and gangster Greek in Connecticut. S38, Ep7
Smokin' Southern Decadence. Guy Fieri samples smoking Southern meats at a family-run barbecue joint in Alabama. Then, he visits Chef G. Garvin's next-level steakhouse in Atlanta. S39, Ep1
Hearty Handfuls. Guy Fieri digs into hearty handfuls of major flavour, like next-level pizza in Tennessee, tasty tacos in Alberta, and elevated empanadas in Georgia. S38, Ep12
Wrapped, Pied And Fried. Guy Fieri has it wrapped, pied and fried with craveable carnitas in Mobile, Alabama, elevated pizza in Cody, Wyoming, and brisket in Omaha, Nebraska. S38, Ep8
Flavor Explosion. Guy Fieri dives into a flavour overload with next-level tacos in Mobile, Alabama, Indian street food in Alberta, Canada, and vegan twists in Los Angeles. S38, Ep9
'Round The World Roundup. Guy Fieri digs into a world of flavours all over the United States! In Georgia, Guy samples Korean chicken wings before heading to Utah for Lebanese food. S29, Ep11
Eating Up New Orleans. Guy Fieri is taking a tasty tour of New Orleans, Louisiana. He stops at a seafood restaurant that serves up creative dishes and tries potato salad with a twist. S29, Ep13

Sandwich-Fest. Guy Fieri checks out how stellar sandwiches stack up with a chicken trifecta in Nebraska, dynamite dip in Alabama and bomb bao in Connecticut. S38, Ep6
Wrapped, Pied And Fried. Guy Fieri has it wrapped, pied and fried with craveable carnitas in Mobile, Alabama, elevated pizza in Cody, Wyoming, and brisket in Omaha, Nebraska. S38, Ep8
Sauced And Spiced. Guy is looking for big flavour. In Tennessee he visits a place serving lamb with Syrian spiced tomato sauce. In Minnesota a café serves wild rice French toast. S10, Ep6
Old Time Attitude. In Nashville, Guy checks out an award-winning restaurant where they have mastered a southern classic. Plus, he heads to a barbecue eatery in Georgia. S9, Ep13
Durham. Adam Richman returns to Durham, North Carolina, for burgers and barbeque, and to recruit a team for the Doughman Challenge: a four-member, food-and-sport relay race. S2, Ep3
Charleston. Adam Richman heads to Charleston, South Carolina for oysters served by the shovel, a fried chicken sandwich smothered in sausage gravy, and a spicy tuna sushi challenge. S2, Ep4
Knoxville. Adam Richman is in Knoxville, Tennessee, trying out some monumental hand-cut steaks, unique burgers and a five-pound barbecue burrito challenge. S2, Ep39
BBQ Bliss. Adam is in Tennessee to taste succulent baby back ribs that have been charcoal smoked for 14 hours. And, he is challenged to finish a humongous smoked meat sandwich. S1, Ep9
Burger Bonanza. Adam travels to North Carolina to try a 20-ounce bacon double-cheeseburger. Later, he is given one hour to finish off the spiciest burger in Texas. S1, Ep15
Charlotte, North Carolina. Casey Webb is in Charlotte, North Carolina, to try three southern specialities. He tries a cheesy chicken dish, a signature sandwich and a huge taco. S4, Ep2
Wilmington, North Carolina. Casey's culinary adventures take him to North Carolina. He tries a BBQ bonanza, a burrito and takes part in the undefeated Pierogi team challenge. S4, Ep1
Lafayette, Louisiana. Casey Webb's epic food adventure takes him to Lafayette, Louisiana. He tries Cajun stew and accepts an ultra-spicy ramen bowl challenge. S4, Ep31
Bari. Adam's in Bari tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including focaccia Barese and a local seafood dish called tiella Barese. S1, Ep9
Rome. Adam's in Rome tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including pasta dish cacio e pepe, and porchetta Romana: suckling pig Rome style. S1, Ep10
James Martin takes a road trip to the country that inspired his cooking career. Travelling in Keith Floyd's motor, he creates delicious new recipes in idyllic locations. S1, Ep19
James Martin takes a road trip to the country that inspired his cooking career. Travelling in Keith Floyd's motor, he creates delicious new recipes in idyllic locations. S1, Ep20
The bakers kick off 1980s Week with a twist on the quiche in their signature. There's a retro treat in the technical and a cool ice cream showstopper. S11, Ep7
It's Dessert Week and the bakers tackle a miniature cheesecake signature. They face a 17th century technical and a showstopping jelly art dessert cake. S11, Ep8
It's Patisserie Week in the semi-final and the bakers' skills are tested in a booze-soaked signature. A delicate showstopper showcases their precision. S11, Ep9
The finalists aim to impress with a set custard and puff pastry signature. With a choccy-nut technical and dessert tower showstopper, who will be crowned winner? S11, Ep10
Rock And Roll. The duo follow in the footsteps of music legends and sample traditional Tennessean dishes at a classic diner. Next, they visit Elvis Presley's mansion in Memphis. S1, Ep3
Cajun. Si and Dave visit the hotbed of Cajun culture in Lafayette, Louisiana. There, the bikers sample crawfish and groove at a jam session. S1, Ep4
